checkpoint_context.active_guardrail_note: This family is admitted only with tighter proof-only guardrails and may not widen itself into live cutover realization, authority handoff, state transfer, trust convergence, or live receipt-pipeline realization.

rehearsal_class_identifiers.description: Rehearse whether a bounded cutover candidate preserves the declared proof obligations needed for a later lawful cutover claim while remaining non-live.
Rehearse whether a candidate respects declared hotswap-boundary legality, including lifecycle posture, state externalization, replay continuity, and refusal posture.
Rehearse whether a candidate stays inside distributed-authority boundary law without claiming authority handoff, transfer, or convergence realization.
Rehearse whether a cutover-shaped candidate remains rollback-aware, refusal-aware, and reconstructable under first-wave bounds.
Record that the rehearsal result is blocked, partial, insufficient, or out of scope.
Rehearse whether the packet preserves explicit non-realization markers so the family cannot widen into live cutover or broader distributed live-ops realization.

evidence_proof_class_identifiers.description: Evidence showing the bounded cutover claim, its declared horizon, its proof packet, and the exact scope of what was or was not rehearsed.
Evidence showing whether lifecycle posture across the rehearsal horizon remains explicit, legal, and non-contradictory.
Evidence showing whether the candidate preserves replay intelligibility, checkpoint intelligibility, and snapshot legality across the rehearsal boundary.
Evidence showing incompatible continuity claims, ambiguous restarts, hidden state, or non-reconstructable runtime posture.
Evidence showing whether operator-transaction, generalized live-cutover receipt, and provenance posture remain reconstructable where required.
Evidence that the correct outcome is refusal, blocked posture, partial posture, or insufficiency rather than affirmative cutover-legality language.
Evidence that a rehearsal packet drifted into live runtime swap claims, authority handoff, transfer, trust execution, publication execution, or other later-wave territory.

blocker_baseline_dependence.gate_freeze_dependency: This family remains subordinate to the Z-P2 admitted_with_cautions gate and the tighter proof-only guardrails frozen by C-ZETA_A_ADMISSION_REVIEW.

runtime: Isolation, coexistence, hotswap boundaries, and distributed-authority foundations remain binding.
No rehearsal packet may hide truth in undocumented local state.
No rehearsal packet may present checkpoint, recover, shard lifecycle, rolling-update, or shard-version substrate as if lawful live cutover were already solved.
release_control: Operator transaction doctrine remains upstream where relevant.
Generalized live-cutover receipts and provenance remain upstream.
Release contract profile, release-index semantics, and exact bounded scope remain reconstructable where required.
Archive presence, mirror visibility, ordinary logs, local traces, and CI output are insufficient as canonical cutover-proof evidence.
trust: Trust-relevant conditions may be rehearsed only within bounded proof-only posture.
Live trust realization, live revocation realization, distributed trust convergence, and publication execution remain unrealized.
Trust-aware rehearsal is not trust realization.

doctrine_admission_rehearsal_realization.doctrine: Defines what bounded runtime cutover proof rehearsal constitutionally means.
doctrine_admission_rehearsal_realization.admission: The family is admitted only with cautions inside the bounded first-wave perimeter.
doctrine_admission_rehearsal_realization.rehearsal: The family may produce bounded cutover-proof, lifecycle-legality, replay/snapshot-compatibility, refusal, and explicit non-realization rehearsal outcomes.
doctrine_admission_rehearsal_realization.realization: Live runtime cutover, deterministic replication, state-partition transfer, distributed trust convergence, live trust/revocation/publication execution, and live cutover receipt-pipeline realization remain outside this family.

invalidity_failure_categories.description: The work drifted into live cutover, transfer, convergence, or execution claims.
Lifecycle posture across the rehearsal horizon is contradictory, hidden, or non-legal.
Replay or snapshot posture is insufficient to support the rehearsal claim.
Continuity expectations conflict with declared runtime, receipt, or boundary posture.
The rehearsal packet is too weak to support a lawful cutover-proof claim.
Canonical operator or generalized receipt/provenance linkage is absent where required.
The rehearsal packet silently turned into trust or revocation realization language.
Only local traces, dashboards, or harness-local evidence were provided.
The candidate belongs to later-wave, blocked, or future-only families instead of this one.
The rehearsal packet is insufficient to support either affirmative bounded rehearsal conclusions or a narrower lawful conclusion.

canonical: upstream checkpoint, blocker, matrix, gate, Z-A0, and Z-A1 doctrine
canonical rehearsal packets, refusal packets, and bounded proof packets later defined under this doctrine
canonical operator transaction and generalized receipt records where relevant

downstream_refinement_prompts: later bounded Ζ-A family doctrine prompts
later broader Ζ planning that must preserve rehearsal versus realization distinctions
